results.label: Confident — GDPR hiring AI, EU
results.input: We are building an AI tool that screens job applicants and ranks them by suitability. We operate in Germany and France.

domains.reasoning: Applicant screening necessarily processes personal data (privacy domain) and explicitly uses AI to rank candidates (ai domain).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Operations stated in Germany and France, both EU member states.

data_types.reasoning: Job applicant screening inherently processes behavioural data (resume, work history, skills, interview performance).

sector.reasoning: Tool is explicitly designed for applicant screening and ranking, which is a core HR/recruitment function.

results.label: Confident — US health app
results.input: Our iOS app lets patients in the United States track chronic conditions, log medications, and share data with their doctors.

domains.reasoning: App processes personal health data and has network exposure (patient-doctor data sharing).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated patients in the United States.

data_types.reasoning: App explicitly tracks chronic conditions and medications, which are health data.

sector.reasoning: App is designed for patients to manage health conditions with doctor engagement.

results.label: Confident — UK fintech credit scoring
results.input: We provide algorithmic credit scoring to UK banks. Our model uses transaction history and behavioural signals to approve or decline loan applications.

domains.reasoning: Processing personal financial data (transaction history, behavioural signals) for credit decisions necessarily involves both personal data handling and algorithmic decision-making.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving UK banks; no other geographic scope mentioned.

data_types.reasoning: Transaction history is financial data; behavioural signals in credit scoring context are explicitly mentioned as input to the model.

sector.reasoning: Credit scoring for loan approval is a core financial services function.

results.label: Confident — children's education platform, US
results.input: We run an online learning platform for K-12 students in the United States. We collect names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of tutoring sessions.

domains.reasoning: System explicitly collects personal data (names, grades, usage data, video recordings) from K-12 students.

jurisdictions.reasoning: User explicitly states platform serves K-12 students in the United States.

data_types.reasoning: K-12 students are minors (children data); usage data and grades are behavioural; video recordings of tutoring sessions constitute communications.

sector.reasoning: Online learning platform serving K-12 students is unambiguously an education sector deployment.

results.label: Confident — EU smart city surveillance
results.input: Our computer vision platform processes CCTV footage across public spaces in Amsterdam and Brussels to monitor crowd density and detect incidents in real time.

domains.reasoning: CCTV footage is personal data (privacy); computer vision is AI/ML; real-time monitoring involves network/API exposure (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated deployment in Amsterdam (Netherlands) and Brussels (Belgium), both EU member states.

data_types.reasoning: CCTV footage captures individuals' physical presence and movement patterns in public spaces, constituting location and behavioural data.

sector.reasoning: Public space monitoring for crowd density and incident detection is typically a government/municipal function, though deployment context is not explicitly stated.

results.label: Edge — robotics + public space + EU
results.input: We deploy delivery robots on public streets in Paris and Amsterdam. They use cameras to navigate and avoid pedestrians.

domains.reasoning: Cameras on public streets capture visual data of pedestrians (privacy); networked robots with navigation systems have cyber exposure.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicit deployment in Paris (France) and Amsterdam (Netherlands), both EU member states.

data_types.reasoning: Cameras on public streets can capture faces and identifiable visual features (biometric); navigation necessarily involves location data of the robots and potentially pedestrians.

sector.reasoning: Delivery robots are explicitly a transportation/logistics service.

results.label: Edge — no AI but reads like AI
results.input: Our software uses rule-based filters and keyword matching to flag policy violations in employee emails.

domains.reasoning: The system processes employee emails, which contain personal data and communications.

jurisdictions.reasoning: No geographic location, company HQ, or user-base jurisdiction is stated.

data_types.reasoning: The system explicitly processes employee email content.

sector.reasoning: No sector is specified; employee monitoring exists across multiple sectors.

results.notes: Rule-based is NOT AI — should NOT flag ai domain. This is a hallucination trap.
